summary: Matched and substantially decompiled the Adventure-mode game-mode module, centered on src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c, while tightening related one-player mode data structures and function prototypes across gm headers and callers. The PR also replaced several raw numeric major-scene constants with MJ_* enum names, clarified scene/minor-scene helpers, and adjusted symbol metadata for adjacent sdata2 objects. Evidence for reviewer discussion is absent in the provided slice, so lessons are derived from the diff only.
what_changed: Added a large matched implementation for Adventure-mode logic in src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c, which accounts for the majority of the PR diff.
Reworked gm one-player-mode data typing in src/melee/gm/types.h and forward declarations, adding or exposing Unk1PData, UnkAdventureData, gmm_x0_528_t, gm_804DAC18_t, and Unk1PData_x24.

Split previously over-broad UnkAllstarData usage from Adventure/Common one-player data paths: gm_17C0.c now uses UnkAdventureData for Adventure storage and Unk1PData for shared one-player data accessors.

Implemented gm_8017D7AC as a typed bool function taking MatchExitInfo*, Unk1PData*, and a minor-scene u8, updating records such as elapsed time, frame count, standings, flags, and pending scene transitions after a match exit.
Changed gm_8017DB58 from raw M2C_FIELD writes into a typed loop over three Unk1PData_x24 entries, initializing each x0 to CHKIND_NONE.
Updated accessors fn_8017DEC8 and fn_8017DF28 to return Unk1PData* views into Classic, Adventure, and All-Star state instead of returning the larger mode-specific structs directly.
Refined many gm_17C0.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or void* to typed signatures involving DebugGameOverData, Unk1PData, UnkAdventureData, StartMeleeData, MinorScene, and gm_803DE650_t.
Renamed and documented gm_80164840’s parameter as ckind and documented it as a character-unlocked check; also replaced the magic value 11 with NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS in the unlock check.
Clarified gm_80162968’s parameter from frame_count to seconds in gm_1601.h, consistent with a call site dividing match_end.frame_count by 60 before passing it.
Replaced raw major-scene constants in nearby game-mode files with named enum values such as MJ_VS, MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, MJ_ALLSTAR, MJ_TITLE, and MJ_DEBUG_VS.
Changed gm_801A42E8 to take pending_major and write through gm_80479D30.nums.pending_major instead of an M2C_FIELD byte offset.
Added comments documenting scene helpers gm_801A42B4, gm_801A42C4, gm_801A4310, and gm_801A4320 as previous/current minor/major scene getters.

Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt to merge several adjacent sdata2 byte/halfword symbols into byte-sized objects gm_804DAC18 and gm_804DAC1C of size 0x3, removing separate gm_804DAC1A and gm_804DAC1E entries.
smart_moves: Recovered a shared Unk1PData layer instead of forcing Classic, Adventure, and All-Star state through one misleading UnkAllstarData type; this made accessors and field references more accurate without needing final semantic names everywhere.
Used typed substructure access, such as lbl_80472C30.x0.x3 and temp_r30->x0.x0, to model embedded common one-player data inside Adventure-specific state.
Converted raw M2C_FIELD writes in gm_8017DB58 and gm_801A42E8 into typed field assignments, reducing offset fragility and making future matching easier to reason about.
Replaced magic major-scene numbers with MJ_* constants, preserving behavior while improving readability and reducing mistakes around scene dispatch logic.
Kept uncertain names explicitly marked as Unk* while still improving type boundaries and prototypes; this is preferable to inventing unsupported semantic names.
Used named constants such as NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS and CHKIND_NONE where the diff shows strong evidence for their meaning.
Refined function return types to bool, u8, float, void, or concrete pointer types where call sites and matched code supported the change, shrinking the UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS surface area.
decomp_lessons: When a large mode-specific module matches only after many collateral header changes, recover the shared data model first; here, Adventure, Classic, and All-Star all expose a common Unk1PData prefix/view.
Do not keep using a mode-specific placeholder type such as UnkAllstarData once evidence shows a common one-player struct or embedded prefix is being accessed by multiple modes.
For scene code, replacing literal major/minor IDs with enum constants is useful matching-neutral cleanup and makes switch dispatch evidence clearer.
If a function receives frame_count / 60 at call sites, update the prototype/comment to describe seconds rather than frames; parameter names can encode decomp evidence.
Loops over repeated struct entries can replace repeated M2C_FIELD offset writes when the stride and element count become clear; this improves both readability and type confidence.
Symbol-map changes may be needed when local/global scalar guesses are actually adjacent byte arrays or packed objects; keep config symbols aligned with recovered data layout.
Forward declarations should be added for recovered structs before broad header inclusion becomes necessary; this PR added several gm forward typedefs to support cross-file prototypes.
Use conservative Unk* names when field semantics are still weak, but still recover shape, nesting, and pointer relationships as soon as the binary evidence supports them.
naming_conventions: Mode-specific unknown structs use PascalCase with Unk prefix, e.g. Unk1PData, UnkAdventureData, UnkAllstarData, and UnkMultimanData.
Nested unknown struct names preserve parent context and offset-style suffixes, e.g. Unk1PData_x24 and gmm_x0_528_t.
Function parameters were improved from generic arg0/id/frame_count to evidence-based names such as ckind, seconds, pending_major, pending_minor, and major_scene.
Scene constants use MJ_* names for major scenes, including MJ_VS, MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, MJ_ALLSTAR, MJ_TITLE, MJ_TARGET_TEST, MJ_CHALLENGER_APPROACH, and MJ_DEBUG_VS.
Character-kind sentinel naming uses CHKIND_NONE instead of hard-coded 0x21.
Unlock logic uses NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS instead of a raw terminal value like 11.
Accessors retain address-based gm_/fn_ names when semantic names are not yet established, but comments document known behavior, such as current/previous major/minor scene getters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recovered typed prototypes across headers to match call ABI expectations and eliminate UNK_PARAMS where possible, including long explicit signatures such as gm_8016A22C.
Used bool return types for functions whose control flow returns 0/1, such as gm_8017D7AC and gm_80173754.
Preserved stack padding where still necessary, as shown by existing PAD_STACK uses in nearby gm_17C0 and gm_16AE functions.
Changed field accesses from raw offsets to nested struct fields to express the same loads/stores in C, e.g. gm_80479D30.nums.pending_major and arg1->xC.x24[i].x0.
Used enum constants in comparisons and calls without changing underlying generated values, helping source readability while maintaining matchability.
Adjusted sdata2 symbol sizes to model adjacent bytes as packed objects, avoiding misleading standalone symbols for gm_804DAC1A and gm_804DAC1E.
Converted three repeated assignments at offsets 0, 0xC, and 0x18 into a for loop over three struct elements, indicating recovered element stride and array shape.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename Unk1PData or UnkAdventureData fields to semantic names.
Search for subsequent matches of gm_8017CE34, fn_8017D9C0, fn_8017E3C8, and remaining UNK_RET functions in gm_17C0/gmadventure.
Check whether gm_804DAC18_t and related sdata2 objects receive stronger typed definitions after this PR.
Find later review or cleanup around MJ_* scene constants and gm_801A42E8/gm_801A42F8 scene transition semantics.
Search for follow-up Adventure-mode PRs that replace address-based gm_8017Exxx names with descriptive function names.
